findings.title: Unused 'ink' color token added to Tailwind config
findings.why_it_matters: A new design token (`ink: '#1e1b4b'`) is added to the Tailwind theme but is never referenced by any class (`text-ink`, `bg-ink`, `border-ink`, etc.) anywhere in the codebase. The same hex value is instead hardcoded directly in styles/globals.css (`color: #1e1b4b;` on html/body). This is speculative configuration: it adds a token to the design system with no consumer, and creates two sources of truth for the same color (the unused token and the duplicated literal). Six months from now a developer editing the body text color will likely miss that `ink` exists and update only the hardcoded hex, or vice versa, letting the two drift apart.
evidence: tailwind.config.js: `colors: { ink: '#1e1b4b' }` added with no matching `ink`-based utility class found anywhere in components/, pages/, or styles/ (grep confirms zero usages of `-ink` classes).
styles/globals.css: `color: #1e1b4b;` on the `html, body` rule duplicates the same hex value as a raw literal instead of applying the new `ink` token via `@apply text-ink`.

residual_risks: The repo's `test` script is just `next build`; there is no automated visual/regression test, so this purely-visual restyle (gradient background, new indigo palette, Space Grotesk headings) relies entirely on manual/visual review to confirm it renders as intended across pages.

The new fixed `body::before` gradient layer and translucent Nav/Card surfaces (bg-white/50, bg-white/80) are layered CSS effects with no automated check that they don't regress on browsers without `inset`/`backdrop` support or in print styles; this is a design-QA concern more than a maintainability defect, but worth a manual pass.
testing_gaps: No automated test verifies that `mdxComponents` (now used directly by pages/blog/[slug].js after removing the local `blogComponents` override) continues to render blog post paragraphs as expected; a manual/visual check of a blog post page is the only verification path.

No test asserts that `next/font/google` (Space Grotesk) loads and the `--font-display` CSS variable resolves correctly in the built output, since `font-display` in tailwind.config.js/globals.css depends on that variable being defined at runtime by Layout.js.
